title: Sundar Pichai faces boos, walkout at Stanford graduation ceremony over Google’s Israel, ICE ties
slides: Sundar Pichai faced boos at Stanford. Why now?
Students walked out over Google’s Israel contract.
The protest also targeted Project Nimbus ties.
ICE immigration work was another flashpoint.
Your next hiring decision may spark similar backlash.
thread_text:
1. Heard the boos?

Stanford students walked out on Sundar Pichai last week, angered by Google’s $1.2 billion Israel contract.

2. Project Nimbus backlash

That cloud deal with Israel gives the military access to Google’s AI tools — students say it crosses a red line.

3. ICE work reignites

Google’s past immigration enforcement contracts are now being dragged back into protest chants at campus events.

4. Backlash spreads to hiring

Hiring at any tech company now risks public walkouts if candidates have ties to defense or surveillance contracts.

5. Your turn to act

Before your next offer letter goes out, search the candidate’s past employer for Project Nimbus involvement. Have you checked?
video_script: Sundar Pichai got booed at Stanford.
Students walked out over Israel contract.
Project Nimbus was a main target.
ICE immigration work also sparked anger.
Your next hire could cause backlash.

instagram.caption:
Students booed and walked out on Sundar Pichai at his own Stanford graduation.

Google's $1.2 billion Project Nimbus contract gives Israel access to their AI tools. Students called that a red line. Past ICE immigration enforcement work was another flashpoint.

🏢 Any hire tied to defense or surveillance contracts now risks public walkouts at your company events.

Before you send your next offer letter, have you searched the candidate's employer for Project Nimbus involvement? 🔎

carousel_slides.description: Sundar Pichai faced boos and a walkout over Google’s $1.2 billion Israel contract and ICE ties.
Students protested Google’s cloud deal giving Israel military access to AI tools, calling it a red line.
Google’s past immigration enforcement work fuels protest chants at university events, reigniting backlash.
Tech hiring risks backlash if candidates have ties to defense or surveillance contracts like Nimbus.

carousel_slides.hook: Save this to check before hiring.
carousel_slides.description: Before sending an offer, search the candidate’s past employer for Project Nimbus involvement.
